title: Microbubbling technique
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8ttud","text":"For use in the protocol \"Large Volume Marine Cyanophage protocols\"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":25,"length":40}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"4C09142208E84FB98E3FA42197C97F7D","url":"https://www.protocols.io/view/Large-Volume-Marine-Cyanophage-Phage-Protocols-c3iykd"}}}}
steps: Inoculate 2L Pro99 with exponentially growing cells (~20-50 ml of dense cells, ~10 8 cells ml -1 )
Let cells grow to about mid 10 7 cells ml -1
Add as much cyanophage stock as available (up to an infective MOI = 3) NOTE: Average cyanophage lysates are ~10^8 - 10^9 SYBR ml^-1; only ~0.1-10% of that is usually infective (as assayed with MPNs)
Adsorb for about 60 minutes without bubbling 01:00:00
Add 1xPro99 amounts of N, P every ~3 days until see 'lysis' (by eye, or more rigorously by a decrease in cell concentration)
Confirm phage production by SYBR titering
creator: Verve Team
